title: semele androgyna subsp. pterygophora
intro: Semele androgyna subsp. pterygophora is a plant species, also known as the pterosparte, belonging to the family Fabaceae. It is a perennial herb native to tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Semele androgyna subsp. pterygophora are typically made from the leaves and roots, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or applied topically as a paste or salve.
